for_kevin_markdown:
## What changed

New in Claude Code: /ultrareview (research preview) runs a fleet of bug-hunting agents in the cloud.

Findings land in the CLI or Desktop automatically. Run it before merging critical changes—auth, data migrations, etc. 

Pro and Max users get 3 free reviews through 5/5. https://t.co/OEzhNHcZdE

## Why it matters

This is most valuable if we can turn it into reusable building blocks quickly, before the underlying capability disappears into stale model knowledge.
for_ua_markdown:
## UA Adoption Package

- Use the linked official source as the canonical reference.
- Materialize multiple implementation primitives, not just a narrative summary.
- Keep the output reusable for UA and standalone Agent SDK projects.
likely_agent_system_value: Likely transferable into standalone agent-system projects.
likely_ua_value: Likely useful for extending UA workflows and agent building blocks.

variants.intent: Translate the capability into reusable UA building blocks.
variants.label: UA Adaptation

primitives.content_markdown:
## UA Adaptation Pattern

- Anchor on the canonical source(s): linked technical references.
- Distill the feature into reusable prompt/workflow/code assets before wiring it into runtime behavior.
- Prefer adding this as a reusable building block for Simone/Cody and future client-system builds.
primitives.rationale: Turn the capability into a repeatable UA integration pattern.
primitives.title: UA adaptation pattern

primitives.content_markdown:
## Workflow Recipe

1. Read the canonical linked source.
2. Extract the behavior contract.
3. Generate prompt/code/workflow primitives.
4. Validate the most promising path in UA.
primitives.rationale: Capture the control flow needed to use the capability repeatedly.
primitives.title: Workflow recipe

variants.intent: Translate the capability into standalone agent-system primitives.
variants.label: Agent SDK Transfer

primitives.content_markdown:
## Agent SDK Adaptation Pattern

- Translate the capability into standalone agent-system patterns, not just IDE assistance.
- Capture the control flow, prompt shape, and code surface required to reproduce the feature in a new project.
- Preserve source provenance so future coding agents can rebuild from official references.
primitives.rationale: Frame the capability for standalone agent systems.
primitives.title: Agent SDK adaptation pattern

primitives.content_markdown:
## Prompt Pattern

```text
Given the official feature docs and any linked demo material, synthesize:
1. the minimal agent behavior contract,
2. the reusable prompt skeleton,
3. the workflow boundaries,
4. the failure modes we should guard against.
```
primitives.rationale: Capture the reusable instruction shape behind the capability.
primitives.title: Prompt pattern

why_now: Recent ClaudeDevs updates suggest this capability is new enough that model cutoffs may miss it, so we should materialize it now.
